rulings.Case Title: FAHIMA ZAHIR VS. 18 LANSING STREET OWNERS'S ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2018, Line 5. DEFENDANT 18 LANSING STREET OWNERS' ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED with leave to amend. The Court notes that Plaintiff asserts at least 5 different theories upon which her "rights" to the decks are based within causes of action for declaratory relief and promissory estoppel. The co-owners of the common areas for which Plaintiff seeks a declaration of rights, are necessary and indispensable parties as the declaration of rights is sought, inter alia, based on prescriptive easements, and equitable easement, easement by estoppel and irrevocable parol license. Plaintiff shall have a 10-day leave to amend to include other HOA members as defendants as to both causes of action to the extent referenced above The issue of indispensable parties has not been reached on prior demurrers. =(501/SRB)

rulings.Case Title: 100 HOOPER FEE OWNER, LLC VS. KR 100 HOOPER, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Kr 100 Hooper Llc And Kilroy Realty L.P. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2018, Line 6. DEFENDANTS KR 100 HOOPER, LLC, KILROY REALTY, L.P.'S Motion For Attorneys Fees. DENIED. While moving party partially obtained the relief sought by its petition to compel arbitration, it is not the prevailing party in this action or proceeding. Recovery of fees under CC 1717 and Section 7 of the Contract is premature. Acosta v. Kerrigan (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 1124 is distinguishable. See Roberts v. Packard, Packard & Johnson (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 822. =(501/SRB)

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS G STEWART VS. STEVEN J FITZPATRICK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion For Order Of Enforcement Of Settlement Agreement And Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Ccp 664.6
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2018, Line 7. PLAINTIFF DENNIS STEWART'S Motion For Order Of Enforcement Of Settlement Agreement And Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To CCP 664.6. DENIED. No competent evidence of breach provided. No authority or contract cited for recovery of attorney's fees incurred for anything other than "enforcement." Additionally, Plaintiff seeks a judgment of possession. It appears, while not made clear, that possession of the premises has already been returned to Plaintiff. =(501/SRB)

rulings.Case Title: LEONARD HO VS. JIN LIN ZHANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys' Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2018, Line 8. DEFENDANTS JINLIN ZHANG, MIAOQIONG QIAN'S Motion For Attorneys' Fees And Costs. DENIED. Defendant fails to cite any authority for the proposition that the Court may award attorney's fees by making a determination as to the prevailing party based on arguments that have not been adjudicated prior to the dismissal. =(501/SRB)

rulings.Case Title: JAYESH PATEL VS. JESSE HANLIN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2018, Line 9. DEFENDANT JESSE HANLIN'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts explaining the inconsistency between allegations in Complaint and First Amended Complaint in good faith. =(501/SRB)
